steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
Since angle $B$ is the right angle, $AC$ is the hypotenuse. Apply the Pythagorean theorem:

$$AC^2 = (10\sqrt{37})^2 + (24\sqrt{37})^2$$

Expand each term:

$$AC^2 = 100 \cdot 37 + 576 \cdot 37$$

Factor out 37:

$$AC^2 = 37(100 + 576) = 37 \cdot 676$$

steps.title: Recognize the perfect square
steps.content:
Observe that $100 + 576 = 676 = 26^2$ (note: $10^2 + 24^2 = 26^2$, a Pythagorean triple). Therefore:

$$AC^2 = 37 \cdot 26^2 = (26\sqrt{37})^2$$

Taking the positive square root:

$$AC = 26\sqrt{37}$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
